_comment: Case studies content. Each item powers a card, a modal, and the /case-studies/[slug] page.

hero.label: Proof of Work
hero.sub: Real briefs, real junctions, real numbers. Filter by format to see how each one was built.

items.title: The IPO the Whole Highway Read
items.summary: 48 premium sites on Mumbai–Pune arteries carried a countdown creative that changed weekly for the six weeks before listing day.
body: Suvarna Finance came to us eight weeks before their IPO with one instruction: every commuter between Mumbai and Pune should know the listing date.

We mapped the morning crawl — the exact junctions where traffic sits longest — and locked 48 front-lit and LED sites facing the slow lanes. The creative ran as a countdown, re-printed and re-installed every Sunday night so Monday's commute always showed a new number.
By listing week, unaided brand recall in intercept surveys had risen 41%. The issue was oversubscribed 3.2 times. The CFO sent the installation crew a crate of pedhas.

quote.text: KP's sites carried our IPO to 20 million commuters. Flawless.
quote.name: Meera Nair

items.title: Nine Cities in 72 Hours
items.summary: An EV scooter launch wrapped 200 buses and 12 metro stations across nine cities — from artwork approval to full installation in three days.
body: Zephyr's launch date was fixed by a national TV spot. Outdoor had to be live the same morning — in nine cities simultaneously.
Our in-house printing units in Nashik and Pune ran around the clock. Installation crews worked in parallel overnight shifts, photographing each wrap at dawn for the proof-of-display dashboard.
The scooter did the commute with its customers for eight weeks. Zephyr tracked 180,000 app installs with OOH-exclusive promo codes.

quote.text: From artwork to installation in 72 hours across 9 cities. Nobody else moves this fast.
quote.name: Sanjana Rao

items.title: The Diwali Screen Takeover
items.summary: Dayparted LED creative that changed with the hour — morning offers for commuters, evening spectacle for families — across Pune and Nagpur.
body: NovaMart's brief was footfall, not fame: get families into 14 stores during the two Diwali weekends.

We ran their entire creative calendar through our LED network's dayparting engine. Office hours showed express-delivery offers; after 6 PM the screens switched to full-motion firework creative synced across every screen in the city centre.
Footfall tracking against the previous year's OOH campaign showed a 3× lift. NovaMart has renewed for every festival window since.

quote.text: 3× the footfall lift of our previous OOH partner. The reporting is best-in-class.
quote.name: Vikram Sethi

items.title: Owning a City Before Opening Day
items.summary: A teaser campaign with no brand name — just an aroma line and a date — turned a café opening into a city-wide guessing game.
body: Café Arabia wanted their Nashik flagship to open famous. We proposed the opposite of a logo blast: three weeks of unbranded black hoardings reading only 'The coffee is coming. 14.09.'
Local press picked up the mystery within days. Radio jockeys ran guessing segments. On reveal night we re-skinned all 18 sites in four hours with the full brand creative.
Opening weekend saw two-hour queues. The campaign cost less than a single week of the radio spots it earned for free.

quote.text: The only vendor who told us NOT to buy a site because the sightline was blocked.
quote.name: Arjun Bhatt

items.title: Public Health at Highway Scale
items.summary: Every KP site in the state carried multilingual vaccination-camp information, updated district-by-district as camp schedules changed.
body: When the state health department needed camp schedules visible in every district, the challenge wasn't creative — it was logistics: 380 sites, 3 languages, schedules changing weekly.

We built a district-level artwork matrix so each site always showed its nearest camp's dates in the dominant local language, and treated schedule changes like LED creative swaps — printed, shipped, and installed inside 48 hours.
District health officers reported camp attendance up 62% in covered talukas versus the previous drive.

quote.text: The rare partner that treats a public-health brief with corporate-launch urgency.
quote.name: Dr. S. Kale

items.title: Selling Sea Views in the Rain
items.summary: Weather-triggered LED creative: when it rained, the screens showed the view from the 40th floor — above the clouds.
body: Monsoon is real estate's dead season. Skyline Realty asked us to make it their launch window.

We wired our Mumbai LED network to a weather API. Clear skies showed standard project creative; the moment rain was detected, screens switched to a shot from the tower's 40th floor — sunlight above the cloud line — with the line 'Live above the weather.'
The campaign booked 340 site visits in eight weeks, and the rain-triggered creative earned national trade-press coverage.

quote.text: They turned the worst season on the calendar into our best quarter.
quote.name: Rahul Wagh
